text: ADMISSION NOTE: ER (10/12/2023 03:15) Patient is a 72-year-old male presenting with acute-on-chronic systolic heart failure exacerbation. Chief complaint of orthopnea, 4-pillow habit, and 15lb weight gain over the last week. PMH: HFrEF (EF 25% by TTE 06/2023), HTN, T2DM, CKD stage III. PE: BP 165/92, HR 108 (sinus tachy), RR 26, SpO2 88% on RA. Notable for JVD to the angle of the jaw, bilateral coarse crackles in bases, 3+ pitting edema to mid-thighs. Labs: NT-proBNP 18,400 pg/mL, Cr 2.1 (baseline 1.6), K 4.2. CXR shows pulmonary edema, cardiomegaly. Plan: Admit to Step-down, start IV Lasix (bolus 80mg), strict I/O, weights, low salt diet. Admission weight: 212 lbs. --- PROGRESS NOTE: Day 1 (10/13/2023 09:00) Patient remains symptomatic. Net negative 1.5L today. IV furosemide continued at 80mg BID. Labs: Cr 2.3, BUN 58, K 3.8. Urine output remains sluggish, ~40mL/hr. Patient reports chest pressure, ruled out for ACS (troponins negative x3). Current weight: 215 lbs. Assessment: ADHF, improving slowly. Continue current regimen. --- PROGRESS NOTE: Day 2 (10/14/2023 09:00) Patient feels slightly improved in breathing. Lung sounds clear to mid-zones. I/O: Net negative 2.2L. Labs: Cr 2.4, BUN 62, K 3.5. Note: Added Carvedilol 6.25mg BID to regimen today (reasoning not documented). Nursing reports patient feeling slightly dizzy with standing. BP 105/65, HR 72. Current weight: 208 lbs. Assessment: Volume status improving. Monitor hemodynamics post-beta-blocker initiation. --- PROGRESS NOTE: Day 3 (10/15/2023 10:00) Patient states he is 'ready to go home.' Denies SOB, but continues to have peripheral edema. Labs: K 3.4 (replace), Mg 1.7. CBC shows mild dilutional anemia. Patient weight recorded by nursing as 218 lbs today, conflicting with the downward trend observed yesterday. Carvedilol continued at 6.25mg BID. Assessment: Preparing for transition to PO Lasix. Final weight: 218 lbs. --- DISCHARGE PLAN (10/16/2023 14:00) Pt stable for discharge to home. Diagnosis: ADHF exacerbated by dietary non-compliance. Medications: Lisinopril 10mg daily, Furosemide 40mg BID, Carvedilol 6.25mg BID (note: beta-blocker was titrated up to 12.5mg BID per nursing bedside report, though pharmacy records show 6.25mg). Follow-up with Cardiology in 1 week. Diet: 2g Sodium restriction. Weight monitoring: daily weights at home. Note: Pending cultures from Day 2 urinalysis and final morning BMP results were not received prior to patient departure and are documented as 'pending' in the EMR. Patient was advised to call primary care for result follow-up.
